The AI that only
asks questions

"I cannot teach anybody anything.
I can only make them think."

— Socrates

Speak or type what's on your mind. Socratic AI responds only with questions that help you discover your own answers.

Download for iOS See how it works

How it works

Three steps to clarity

No advice. No answers. Just the right questions at the right time.

01

Share what's on your mind

Speak or type a decision, belief, dilemma, or anything you're wrestling with. Voice-first design means you can think out loud.

02

The AI asks, never tells

Socratic AI responds only with questions. It challenges your assumptions, explores consequences, and helps you see what you already know.

03

Discover your own answers

Star your breakthrough moments. Capture clarity summaries. Build a personal journal of the wisdom you've uncovered through your own thinking.

Features

Everything you need to think deeper

Built for real self-discovery, not surface-level affirmations.

🎙

Voice-first conversations

Tap and speak. Voice input is the default because speaking your thoughts changes how you think. Keyboard is always available too.

🧭

Guided Paths

Structured Socratic journeys for life's biggest questions. Navigate decisions, examine beliefs, set boundaries, and find purpose.

⭐

Breakthrough moments

Star the questions and realizations that hit hardest. Your breakthroughs are saved to your journal and can be shared as insight cards.

📖

Insight Journal

Every session generates a clarity summary. Browse your insights by topic, favorite the ones that matter, and track your growth over time.

☀️

Daily Socratic questions

Start each day with a question designed to spark reflection. Write a quick thought or go deeper with a full session.

🔗

Shareable insight cards

Turn your breakthroughs into beautifully branded cards. Share your realizations on social media or save them to your camera roll.

Five thinking lenses

Choose how you explore

Each lens shapes the questions you receive, bringing a different philosophical tradition to your self-discovery.

Stoic

Focus on what you can control

Existentialist

Embrace radical freedom

Pragmatist

What works in practice

Eastern

Find harmony and balance

Neutral

Open and balanced approach

"To find yourself, think for yourself."

— Socrates
